steps.title: Spot the clause types
steps.content: On the left of the blank: “Although most meteorites are fragments of asteroids” is a dependent clause—it starts with “Although,” a subordinating conjunction, and cannot be a complete sentence on its own. On the right: “several hundred have been identified as being from the Moon or Mars” is an independent clause—it has a subject and verb and can stand alone.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule
steps.content: When a dependent clause comes first, use a comma to separate it from the independent clause that follows. Choice B correctly places a comma here. The other choices violate this rule: A creates a sentence fragment by ending with a period, C misuses a semicolon (which requires two independent clauses, not one dependent and one independent), and D misuses a colon (which introduces lists or elaborations, not contrasting information). That matches choice B — B is correct.
